message:
Add keyboard symbol reference and fix ASCII diagram alignment

Two improvements to guide accessibility and formatting:

1. Keyboard Symbol Reference (new section)

   Added a reference table under "What You'll Need" that maps macOS keyboard
   symbols to their key names and Windows/third-party keyboard equivalents:

   | Symbol | Mac Key   | Windows/Third-Party |
   |--------|-----------|---------------------|
   | ⌃      | Control   | Ctrl                |
   | ⌥      | Option    | Alt                 |
   | ⌘      | Command   | Windows key (⊞)     |
   | ⇧      | Shift     | Shift               |

   This helps users who:
   - Are new to macOS and unfamiliar with ⌃⌥⌘⇧ symbols
   - Use third-party keyboards (mechanical, Windows layout) where physical
     key labels don't match macOS terminology
   - Need to translate shortcuts like "⇧⌃ ←" to "Shift+Ctrl+Left Arrow"

   Includes an example showing that ⌥⌘ → on a third-party keyboard means
   "Alt + Windows + Right Arrow".

2. ASCII Diagram Spacing Fix

   The nested box diagram in "Working with Remote Servers" had a one-character
   misalignment on the tmux window line:

   Before: │  │  │  │  [Window 1] [Window 2]   │  │  │  │  (46 chars)
   After:  │  │  │  │  [Window 1] [Window 2]    │  │  │  │  (47 chars)

   Added one space after "[Window 2]" so all lines in the diagram are
   consistently 47 characters wide, maintaining proper box alignment.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -76,6 +76,19 @@ This works well because:
 
 > **Note:** I have no affiliation with BetterMouse. It's the best tool I've found for this; Logitech's own software (Logi Options+) cannot map the thumbwheel to arbitrary keyboard shortcuts.
 
+### Keyboard Symbol Reference
+
+This guide uses macOS keyboard symbols. Here's a quick reference:
+
+| Symbol | Mac Key | Windows/Third-Party Keyboard |
+|--------|---------|------------------------------|
+| `⌃` | Control | Ctrl |
+| `⌥` | Option | Alt |
+| `⌘` | Command | Windows key (⊞) |
+| `⇧` | Shift | Shift |
+
+So `⇧⌃ ←` means **Shift + Control + Left Arrow**, and `⌥⌘ →` means **Option + Command + Right Arrow** (or **Alt + Windows + Right Arrow** on third-party keyboards).
+
 ---
 
 ## Part 1: Setting Up BetterMouse
